name: Arrow
summary: Pure speed fighter. Fastest SCM and highest pitch rate of any light fighter. Wins by dictating engagement range and choosing when to pass. Cannot trade sustained hits — one S1 shield means you die if caught.
strengths: 75°/s pitch — hardest to track of any fighter
229 SCM — fastest escape and re-engagement
Tiny cross section — hard to hit at range
weaknesses: 3,168 HP shield — no margin for error
Only 2× S2 missiles — minimal missile threat
Punishing if opponent survives first pass

builds.label: Fixed Deadbolt
builds.w: 3× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: Open every pass with missiles + full burst. Stay under 800m. Do not extend beyond 1.5km — drops below Deadbolt projectile speed.

builds.label: Panther Energy
builds.w: 3× CF-337 Panther S3
builds.note: Energy sustain for longer fights. Stealth QD for cold arrival — first pass catches opponents unaware.

counter: Gladius (out-shields it), Buccaneer (out-alphas the opener)

name: Gladius
summary: Most forgiving light fighter. Dual engines give real survivability the Arrow lacks. Twice the shield HP. Best ship to develop PvP fundamentals — the meta benchmark everything else is measured against.
strengths: 6,336 HP — double the Arrow at light class
Dual engines — survive one shield break and disengage
Versatile range — effective at 300m or 800m
weaknesses: Lower pitch (68) vs Arrow (75) — loses knife fights
Less alpha than Buccaneer — can get out-burst
No cargo, no utility — pure fighter

builds.label: Deadbolt Meta
builds.w: 3× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: Standard meta build. FR-66 shield is the priority upgrade. Aim for 400-700m passes. Let the shield absorb one mistake before disengaging.

builds.label: Panther Sustain
builds.w: 3× CF-337 Panther S3
builds.note: Better for extended sessions or defensive opponents. Panthers sustain indefinitely vs Deadbolt ammo limits.

counter: Buccaneer (higher burst), Sabre (stealth approach negates reaction time)

name: Buccaneer
summary: Highest alpha burst of any light fighter. Lowest shield HP. Wins the opening exchange or dies. The convergence between S4 ventral, S3 chin, and S1 nose requires practice — they angle differently at different ranges.
strengths: Highest alpha in class — S4+2×S3+2×S1 simultaneously
240 SCM — fastest escape speed of any light fighter
4× S3 missiles — larger opening salvo than Arrow or Gladius
weaknesses: 2,160 HP — dies to one good pass
49 pitch — worst turning rate of light fighters
Weapon convergence requires significant practice

builds.label: Full Alpha Deadbolt
builds.w: Deadbolt IV S4 + 2× Deadbolt III S3 + 2× Deadbolt I S1
builds.note: Fire everything simultaneously — missiles first, then all guns. At 400m the convergence lines up on medium+ targets. Disengage immediately after each pass. You cannot absorb return fire.

counter: Anything that survives the opening burst

name: Sabre
summary: Lowest IR of any human fighter (3,844). Stealth component stack keeps EM below most scanner thresholds. First pass catches opponents mid-manoeuvre. 4× S3 simultaneously is the highest alpha at S3 size class.
strengths: IR 3,844 — lowest of any human fighter, hardest missile lock
4× S3 simultaneously — highest S3-level burst in game
Stealth stack legal — run cold until you choose to engage
weaknesses: S1 shield only — thin for a medium
Levski only — Teach's Ship Shop
EM spikes on firing — stealth advantage ends once guns fire

builds.label: Full Stealth Alpha
builds.w: 4× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: Arrive cold — EM under scanner threshold. Open with Tempests + full Deadbolt burst before opponent radar locks. ~800+ alpha on first pass before target reacts. Disengage if they survive — your advantage is spent.

builds.label: Panther Sustained
builds.w: 4× CF-337 Panther S3
builds.note: Energy for evasive opponents. Panther has better range. Still opens stealth.

counter: Ghost Mk II (out-stealths), Banu Defender (alien shield absorbs alpha)

name: Ghost Mk II
summary: IR 1,693 — stealthiest human fighter in 4.8. EM 6,083 idle is below most passive scanner thresholds. Arrives genuinely cold. 2× S4 hits harder per shot than 3× S3. The asymmetric threat — opponents do not know it is there until guns fire.
strengths: IR 1,693 — by far lowest of any human fighter
EM 6,083 idle — invisible to most passive scanners with stealth stack
S4 guns hit harder per shot — fewer guns, higher damage each
weaknesses: Only 2 gun slots — lower sustained DPS than Sabre or Gladius
4× S1 missiles — cannot force meaningful evasion
Pledge only

builds.label: Full Stealth
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt IV S4
builds.note: Approach from blind spot, fire at sub-600m before radar lock. S4 Deadbolts land 455+ alpha per shot. Disengage if first pass fails — your advantage is information asymmetry, not sustained firepower.

counter: Active pinging counters the stealth. Banu Defender alien shield absorbs the burst.

name: F7C Mk II
summary: Most versatile medium fighter. S3/S4/S5 hardpoints — upgrade S5 first, it dominates the loadout. 8× S3 missiles is the largest missile package of any medium. High EM is visible but the shield and firepower make up for it.
strengths: S5 centre slot — Deadbolt V is the highest alpha single gun at this class
8× S3 missiles — enormous opening salvo
6,336 HP shield comparable to Gladius on a medium frame
weaknesses: High EM 30,708 — visible on passive scanners
Lower pitch (52) — less agile than Arrow or Sabre
Expensive to fully upgrade — S5 Deadbolt V priority first

builds.label: Deadbolt Meta
builds.w: Deadbolt V S5 + 2× Deadbolt IV S4 + M3A S3
builds.sh: FR-76 M crafted or Asclepius S2
builds.note: S5+2×S4 combined burst is devastating. M3A on nose for energy sustain between Deadbolt reloads. Open with all 8 missiles then guns. FR-76 M from Foxwell Red is the key shield upgrade.

counter: Sabre or Ghost (stealth approach vs high EM), Arrow (out-manoeuvres at close range)

name: Shiv
summary: 4.8 value king. 7,200 HP M-shield, 2× S4 pilot + remote turret, 32 SCU cargo, bed. The remote turret adds genuine DPS opponents underestimate. Best in-game purchasable ship at 5.5M aUEC.
strengths: 7,200 HP M-shield — tankiest ship at this price point
Remote turret adds S3 DPS pilot controls simultaneously
32 SCU cargo — loot every kill for passive income
weaknesses: Medium frame — less agile than light fighters
High EM from utility components
Remote turret aiming separate to pilot guns requires practice

builds.label: Solo All-rounder
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt IV S4 + 2× M3A S3 remote
builds.sh: FR-76 M crafted or Asclepius M
builds.note: S4 Deadbolts strip armor, M3A remote sustains. 10 missiles for the opener. M-shield absorbs mistakes. Loot components after each kill — 10-50k each at ERT.

builds.label: Duo PvP
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt IV S4 + gunner: 2× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: With a gunner the Shiv rivals ships 3× the price. Pilot on primary target, gunner on flankers.

counter: Sabre or Ghost (stealth approach), Arrow swarm (fast passes overwhelm shield before turret responds)

name: Banu Defender
summary: Alien tech changes the rules. Banu shields regenerate from 0% with no delay penalty unlike human shields. 4× S3 tachyon cannons are long-range high-alpha energy weapons. Hard counter to burst alpha builds that expect shield regen delay.
strengths: Alien shield — regenerates from 0% with no delay
4× S3 Tachyon — high alpha energy, best at 600-1200m
Shield mechanic negates the alpha-burst meta
weaknesses: Pledge only
Tachyon cannons require held fire — skill dependent
High EM 26,946 — visible at range

builds.label: Tachyon Sustained
builds.w: 4× Tachyon S3 stock — do not swap
builds.sh: Stock Banu shield — do not replace
builds.note: Do NOT swap the alien Banu shield — unique regen mechanics not replicated by human shields. Tachyon range is 600-1200m. The shield mechanic means opponents who deplete it must wait — it does not wait.

counter: Sustained distortion weapons bypass regen advantage. Ghost Mk II before it can engage.

name: Scorpius
summary: Best value PvP heavy at 5.17M. 10,560 HP M-shield. 7 pilot hardpoints. 230 SCM is fast for a heavy. 8× S4 missiles make the opening devastating. Low pitch (42) means win through firepower and shield, not manoeuvring.
strengths: 10,560 HP M-shield — absorbs multiple passes
230 SCM — fastest heavy fighter by SCM
7 pilot guns + 8× S4 missiles — highest hardpoint count at class
weaknesses: 42 pitch — worst agility of any meta fighter
High EM 28,043 — easy missile lock at range
Remote turret requires separate aiming input

builds.label: ERT Alpha
builds.w: 3× Deadbolt IV S4 + 4× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: Open with all 8× S4 missiles — anything that survives faces 7 guns. FR-76 M is the upgrade that makes this a tank. ERT stacking with this build = 1M+ aUEC/hr.

counter: Multiple fast lights (Arrow/Gladius swarm exploits 42-pitch), stealth approach vs high EM

name: Asgard
summary: 21,120 HP M-shield. 16× S3 missiles — largest light missile package of any fighter. The worst agility (32 pitch, 4.2s to SCM) means it is a mobile gun platform, not a dogfighter. It does not dodge. It absorbs and fires overwhelming salvos.
strengths: 21,120 HP M-shield — extraordinary punishment absorption
16× S3 missiles — devastating opening salvo
S5+4×S4+S3 — exceptional firepower once in range
weaknesses: 32 pitch — worst agility of any meta ship
4.2s to SCM — slow acceleration, bad escape speed
IR 11,632 / EM 47,339 — extremely visible

builds.label: ERT Sustained
builds.w: Deadbolt V S5 + 4× Deadbolt IV S4 + M3A S3
builds.mis: Ignite III × 16 — fire all on approach
builds.note: 16 missiles open every fight. FR-76 M regen is critical — this ship takes hits and recovers. Do not dodge — use size to absorb and firepower to overwhelm. Medical Ursa fits in cargo bay for bunker ops respawn.

counter: Sustained distortion over time, fast lights at knife range where S2 agility cant respond

name: Vanguard Harbinger
summary: The ERT tank. 21,120 HP dual M-shields. Bed for log-out at the fight. Enormous missile package — 3× S5 Ignites alone kill most targets. Not a dogfighter. Built for extended sessions and survivability, not agility.
strengths: 21,120 HP dual M-shield — effectively unkillable short-term
3× S5 + S3/S2 racks — devastating opening package
Bed bay — log out at fight location, no quantum travel reset
weaknesses: 41 pitch — cannot out-turn anything
IR 11,580 / EM 31,464 — extremely visible
S2 chin guns weak without the S5 nose to complement

builds.label: ERT Grind
builds.w: Deadbolt V S5 + 4× CF-007 S2
builds.sh: 2× FR-76 M crafted — both slots priority
builds.mis: Ignite V × 3 + Ignite III × 6 + Ignite II × 8
builds.note: Open every ERT with full missile package — 3× S5 Ignites often kill before guns. Dual FR-76 M is what makes this feel unkillable. For PvP: stay at range, fire missiles, avoid knife fights.

counter: Sustained distortion over time, multiple fast lights at close range

name: Meteor
summary: Alpha king. Locked S5 Leonid Ballistic Cannons define the ship — two of the hardest-hitting guns in the medium class. 229 SCM fast for a heavy loadout. In-game purchasable at ~4.31M aUEC. Highest theoretical opening alpha of any medium fighter.
strengths: 2× locked S5 Leonid Ballistic Cannons — unique, cannot be matched
229 SCM — very fast for this firepower level
4× S3 flex adds substantial DPS on top of locked Leonids
weaknesses: S2 shield only despite medium frame — fragile
S5 Leonids ballistic — limited ammo, slow projectile speed
Locked S5 guns cannot be swapped — no loadout flexibility on primary guns

builds.label: PvE Deadbolt
builds.w: Locked Leonids + 4× Deadbolt III S3
builds.note: Leonids strip armor, Deadbolts finish. Fire everything simultaneously — missiles + Leonids + S3 at once. Highest opening burst of any medium. Manage ammo.

builds.label: PvP Mixed
builds.w: Locked Leonids + 2× CF-337 Panther + 2× M3A S3
builds.note: Energy flex for PvP sustain. Leonids punch through shields, energy guns sustain after. The S1/S2 shield is the limitation — cannot tank return fire.

counter: Ghost Mk II stealth burst before Meteor fires, Banu Defender alien shield absorbs opener

name: F8C Lightning
summary: Six pilot guns converging on a single point. The F8C's defining feature is not the shield or the speed — it is the simultaneous burst of 2× S3 nose and 4× S2 wing all converging at 400-600m. Pledge only. When it works, nothing in the medium class survives the opener.
strengths: 6 pilot guns — highest simultaneous alpha at this size class
14,960 HP M-shield — real survivability unlike the Sabre or Ghost
210 SCM fast for a 6-gun platform — can dictate engagement range
weaknesses: Pledge only — not accessible in-game
2× S3 + 4× S2 split means ammo management across 2 types
S1 cooler slot limits thermal headroom on sustained fights

builds.label: ERT Deadbolt
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt III S3 nose + 4× Deadbolt II S2 wing
builds.sh: FR-76 M crafted or Asclepius M
builds.note: The 6-gun convergence at 400-600m is the win condition. Fire all 8 missiles on approach then all 6 guns simultaneously. FR-76 M is the priority upgrade. Manage S3/S2 ammo separately — they deplete at different rates.

builds.label: PvP All-Energy
builds.w: 2× M3A S3 nose + 4× M2A S2 wing
builds.note: Full energy — no ammo management. Lower alpha than Deadbolts but infinite sustain. Use Tempests to force breaks, then 6-gun while they flare.

counter: Ghost Mk II (stealth approach before F8C sees it), Banu Defender (alien shield survives opener)

name: Hurricane
summary: Budget heavy fighter at 9.23M aUEC. 10,560 HP M-shield. 2× S4 pilot plus a remote turret that adds a third S4 when solo. Cheapest path to M-shield and S4 pilot guns. Outclassed by Vanguard and Scorpius but costs a fraction of the price.
strengths: 10,560 HP M-shield — real heavy fighter tankiness at budget price
3× S4 effective solo (pilot + remote) — strong combined DPS
9.23M aUEC — most accessible heavy fighter in 4.8
weaknesses: 48 pitch — cannot manoeuvre with mediums let alone lights
4× S2 only — tiny missile package for a heavy fighter
S1 power/cooler limits component ceiling

builds.label: Solo ERT
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt IV S4 pilot + Deadbolt IV S4 remote
builds.sh: FR-76 M crafted or Asclepius M
builds.note: Three S4 Deadbolts is serious alpha. Solo: remote runs pilot-controlled. FR-76 M is the upgrade that matters most. Stack ERT contracts and absorb damage.

builds.label: Duo Grind
builds.w: Pilot: 2× M4A S4 energy + gunner: Deadbolt IV S4 remote
builds.note: With a gunner the remote becomes independent. Pilot on energy for no ammo management, gunner on ballistic for alpha. Assign gunner to spot flankers.

counter: Multiple fast lights (48 pitch cannot respond to swarm), Sabre/Ghost stealth approach

name: Guardian MX
summary: 4× S4 pilot guns with a 21,120 HP M-shield on a solo heavy frame. 24× S2 missiles — three full 8-missile salvos. Pledge only. Strong entry in the heavy fighter bracket with a unique all-S4 pilot layout.
strengths: 4× S4 simultaneously — strong sustained pilot DPS
21,120 HP M-shield — equal to the Vanguard
24× S2 missiles — fire 3 full salvos before running dry
weaknesses: Pledge only
No S5 nose gun — lower per-shot alpha than Vanguard Harbinger
Dual cooler slots (M+S1) — unusual layout

builds.label: ERT Sustained
builds.w: 4× Deadbolt IV S4
builds.sh: FR-76 M crafted or Asclepius M
builds.mis: Ignite II × 24 — fire in salvos of 8
builds.note: 4× S4 Deadbolts is a brutal sustained DPS package. Fill both cooler slots — M-slot first. Open every fight with 8 missiles then 4-gun sustained. M-shield means you absorb mistakes.

builds.label: PvP Energy
builds.w: 4× M4A S4 laser cannon
builds.note: Full energy sustain. 4× M4A is very consistent DPS with no ammo pressure. The heavy shield and relatively agile frame for its size makes it competitive against mediums. Fire Tempests in pairs.

counter: Fast lights at knife range (55 pitch limits response), sustained distortion over time

name: Ares Ion
summary: Single fixed S7 laser cannon. One shot from the SF7E strips most fighter shields entirely. Designed as a capital-killer that can threaten fighters at range. Pledge only. The most specialist ship in the game — devastating in its role, helpless outside it. Needs fighter escort at all times.
strengths: S7 Laser Cannon — single highest alpha pilot gun in game
21,120 HP M-shield — absorbs punishment while lining up shots
12× S5 missiles — supplemental capital deterrent
weaknesses: 38 pitch — worst agility of any meta ship
Single fixed gun — zero sustained DPS if target evades
Pledge only — specialist ship, not a daily driver

builds.label: Capital Killer
builds.w: S7 SF7E Laser (locked — cannot swap)
builds.sh: Asclepius M or FR-76 M crafted
builds.note: The S7 IS the build. Component upgrades are the only variable. Maximise shield and power output. Fire at capital-class and large targets. Against fighters: stay at 1-2km and let the S7 range advantage work. NEVER solo dogfight. Needs 2+ fighter escorts at all times.

counter: Any agile fighter inside 400m — the S7 cannot track fast targets at knife range.

name: Redeemer
guns: 2× S4 pilot + 2× S4 turret + 2× S3 turret
summary: Org gunship. 2× S4 pilot plus 4 crew-operated turrets. With full crew it puts out more combined DPS than ships three times its price. 21,120 HP M-shield on a medium frame. Solo it is a competent heavy fighter — with 3-4 crew it is a fleet engagement platform.
strengths: 6 total gun emplacements across pilot + 4 crew turrets — staggering combined DPS
21,120 HP M-shield — genuine tanking capability
L-grade power/cooler in a medium hull — rare component ceiling
weaknesses: Pledge only
40 pitch — relies on firepower over manoeuvre
Solo: 4 turret slots wasted — half the platform unused

builds.label: Full Crew Org
builds.w: Pilot: 2× M4A S4 + turrets: Deadbolt IV S4 × 2 + M3A S3 × 2
builds.note: Assign roles before undocking. Pilot calls targets, shield op manages power triangle, 2 gunners on S4 turrets. Combined DPS rivals capital-adjacent platforms. L-grade power despite medium hull is the Redeemer's secret.

builds.label: Solo ERT
builds.w: 2× Deadbolt IV S4 pilot — turrets unmanned
builds.note: Solo viable thanks to M-shield and good pilot DPS. Much better with even one gunner on an S4 turret. Treat it as a heavy fighter with L-grade internals.

counter: Fast light swarm (40 pitch cannot respond to multiple approach vectors), sustained distortion

name: Starlancer TAC
guns: 4× S4 pilot + 2× S4 turret + S3 belly
summary: L-shield on a medium hull. 144,000 HP shield is extraordinary — more than most large ships. 4× S4 pilot guns before turrets even fire. Pledge only. The Starlancer TAC does not manoeuvre, it does not run — it absorbs and overwhelms. An entirely different engagement model.
strengths: 144,000 HP L-shield — absurd tanking for a medium frame
4× S4 pilot simultaneously — serious DPS before turrets contribute
L-grade power/cooler — same internals as large ships
weaknesses: Pledge only
35 pitch / 150 SCM — cannot escape or reposition
IR 12,000 / EM 38,000 — extremely visible at all ranges

builds.label: ERT Tank
builds.w: 4× Deadbolt IV S4 pilot + turrets: Deadbolt IV S4 × 2 + M3A S3
builds.sh: Asclepius L or FR-76 L crafted
builds.note: 144,000 HP L-shield means you absorb punishment that kills everything else. Fly straight — you do not dodge. 4× S4 pilot sustained plus turret DPS from crew. FR-76 L is the end-game shield upgrade.

builds.label: Solo ERT
builds.w: 4× M4A S4 laser cannon
builds.note: Solo: turrets unmanned but 4× S4 energy pilot guns sustain indefinitely. Fly straight at ERTs — the L-shield absorbs everything. The ship effectively negates the need to dodge at ERT tier.

counter: Sustained distortion weapons over time, fast lights at knife range (35 pitch cannot respond)

name: Perseus
summary: 4.8 in-game purchasable (~40M aUEC). The Perseus is an anti-capital gunship with 2× manned S7 turrets — the hardest-hitting crewed guns in the game. 211,200 HP L-shield. Not a fighter, not a frigate — a platform that turns capital-class firepower over to a 3-crew team. At 40M it is the most expensive in-game purchasable ship in 4.8.
strengths: 2× manned S7 turrets — devastating at any range against large targets
211,200 HP L-shield — capital-class survivability
20× S5 torpedoes — anti-capital deterrent package
weaknesses: 40M aUEC — enormous investment requiring extended grind
20 pitch — cannot reposition, cannot escape
IR 18,000 / EM 52,000 — visible across the entire system

builds.label: Anti-Capital Fleet
builds.w: Turret gunners: S7 ballistic or laser (locked) × 2
builds.mis: S5 torpedoes × 20 — fire at capital targets from max range
builds.note: The Perseus does not have pilot guns — it is entirely crew-dependent. Minimum crew: 1 pilot + 2 S7 turret gunners. Pilot manages navigation, shields, and power. Gunners call targets and fire independently. Against fighters the S7 turrets are overkill — use the torpedoes as a deterrent. The Perseus is at its best in fleet ops against Hammerheads, Idris, or large station targets.

builds.label: Defensive Config
builds.w: All turret slots filled — S7 manned + defensive turrets covered
builds.note: Fill every defensive turret with the best available. With a 5+ crew the Perseus is virtually unkillable by anything short of another capital. Run in org fleet ops as the fire support anchor — let fighters screen while the Perseus absorbs damage and S7 turrets clear priority targets.

counter: Light fighter swarm overwhelming defensive coverage, sustained distortion on the L-shield, interdiction + boarding before crew can respond

name: Hammerhead
summary: The Hammerhead is the anti-fighter fleet ship. 6× manned S4 turrets with overlapping coverage fields — every vector is covered. 144,000 HP L-shield. At ~45.5M aUEC it is the second most expensive in-game purchasable ship in 4.8. With a full 7-crew it creates a kill zone no fighter can survive. The defining org fleet ship.
strengths: 6× S4 manned turrets with full spherical coverage — no blind spots with full crew
144,000 HP L-shield — same as Starlancer TAC but on a larger hull
32× S3 missiles — devastating opening salvo against fighter formations
weaknesses: ~45.5M aUEC — the biggest in-game investment in 4.8
22 pitch / 160 SCM — completely static, no repositioning
Requires full 7-crew to be effective — unmanned turrets are dead weight

builds.label: Full Crew Anti-Fighter
builds.w: 6× Deadbolt IV S4 across all turrets
builds.mis: Ignite III × 32 — fire all sectors simultaneously against fighter formations
builds.note: Assign turret sectors before undocking: bow, stern, port, starboard, dorsal, ventral. Coordinator (pilot or dedicated role) calls priority targets and sectors. The overlapping turret coverage means anything within 2km is in crossfire from at least 2 turrets simultaneously. FR-76 L shield is the priority upgrade — the L-shield is what keeps the ship alive while turrets work.

builds.label: 4-Crew Reduced
builds.w: 4× Deadbolt IV S4 on primary sectors (bow/stern/port/starboard)
builds.note: 4 crew leaves dorsal/ventral uncovered — fighters can attack from above/below. Still very effective but dorsal/ventral blind spots must be covered by escort fighters. Do not run the Hammerhead with fewer than 4 crew in a contested environment.

counter: Dorsal/ventral approach with fewer than 6 crew, sustained distortion on L-shield over time, coordinated torpedo strike from outside turret range

name: RSI Polaris
guns: 6× manned turrets + remote nose + auto rear
missiles: 4× S10 torpedo tubes (60 torps) + 16× S3 + 12× S2
summary: Pledge-only corvette-class capital ship. The Polaris is unique in having a surprisingly low IR signature for its size — lower than the Constellation, MSR and 400i. 210 SCM fast for a capital. 4× S10 torpedo tubes are its defining weapon — each torp can obliterate a fighter formation or strip capital shields in seconds. Hangar bay fits one medium fighter. Medical bay, brig, cargo hold. The apex Catfight fleet asset.
strengths: 4× S10 torpedo tubes — highest alpha damage delivery in the game
210 SCM — extraordinarily fast for a capital ship, can disengage unlike Hammerhead/Perseus
IR signature lower than many medium ships — approaches cold before detection
weaknesses: Pledge only — the rarest ship in most orgs
12 pitch — cannot fight agile fighters directly, relies entirely on turrets and torps
Full effectiveness requires 10+ crew — pilot, coordinator, 6 turret gunners, torpedo officer, engineer

builds.label: Full Fleet Engagement
builds.w: 6× turret gunners on manned S4/S5 turrets + torpedo officer on 4× S10 tubes
builds.mis: 60× S10 torps pre-loaded + 16× S3 rack + 12× S2 rear
builds.note: Crew positions: Pilot (navigation, shield triangle), Coordinator (calls targets, manages engagement range), Torpedo Officer (fires S10 salvos, tracks reload), 6× Turret Gunners (sector assignments), Engineer (power allocation, damage control). The Polaris fights at 3-10km range — torpedoes engage capitals, turrets handle fighters. Never close to knife range.

builds.label: Stealth Approach
builds.w: All weapons powered down until within 5km of target
builds.sh: Powered down on approach — spike only on engagement
builds.mis: Full torp load — fire full salvo at once
builds.note: The Polaris's low IR makes stealth approaches viable for a capital ship. Approach cold, power up within 5km, fire full S10 salvo before the target can respond. Against a Hammerhead this eliminates the turret advantage entirely — dead before they react. Coordinate with escort fighters to cover the approach.

counter: Sustained fighter swarm at knife range (12 pitch cannot track), distortion weapons on shields, interdiction + boarding before torpedo reload
